title: Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi
content:
{{Top|rwm|ncl|reb|inf|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi}} {{Noncanon|real=1}} {{ComicSeries |image=[[File:Infin_rotjtpb.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Infinities:
Return of the Jedi'' |writer=[[Adam Gallardo]] |penciller=[[Ryan Benjamin]] |inker=[[Saleem Crawford]] |letterer=[[Michael David Thomas]] |colorist=[[Joel Benjamin]] |cover artist=[[Rodolfo Migliari]] |editor= |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |start date=[[November 6]], [[2003]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/00-224/Star-Wars-Infinities---Return-of-the-Jedi-1-of-4|text=Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 1}} |end date=[[March 31]], [[2004]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/00-234/Star-Wars-Infinities---Return-of-the-Jedi-4-of-4|text=Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 4}} |schedule=Monthly |format=Completed |issues=4 |timeline= |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi''''' is a 4-part [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] sub-series of ''[[Star Wars Infinities]]''. It was published from [[November]] [[2003]] to November 20, 2004, then compiled as a trade paperback on March 31, [[2004]]. It deals with what could have happened following the chaos of failed negotiations between [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] and "[[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]]." ==Plot summary== At the palace of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], disguised as the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]], is making her offer to Jabba of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] in an attempt to gain the Hutt's trust. But things go horribly wrong. When [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] is knocked down by Jabba following her demand of "fifty thousand, no less", the droid's head comes off, leaving her with no means to communicate with the Hutt. With no translator and forced to reveal herself to the entire assembly, she pulls the [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] and threatens to use it unless Jabba cooperates to release Solo. [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], noticing [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] drawing his weapon, knocks him aside, sending his shot into Leia's elbow, accidentally activating the thermal detonator. As the crowd scatters, Fett takes the carbonite block of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] away and Leia and Chewbacca chase after him. Leia attempts to stop him, but is unsuccessful, one shot accidentally hitting the block's control panel and two other shots missing him. With no other option, she, Lando, and Chewbacca escape the palace shortly before it blows up when the thermal detonator goes off; C-3PO is destroyed in the explosion and Jabba, [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]], and [[Salacious B. Crumb/Legends|Salacious Crumb]] are killed. Boba Fett brings Solo aboard the ''Slave I'' and flees Tatooine. Commandeering the [[Khetanna/Legends|''Khetanna'']], they meet with Luke and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] in the desert and decide to search the local smuggler hangouts in order to find out where Fett has gone. Luke promises Leia that he will find Han. Onboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] meets the arriving [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who orders Vader to stay near him, as events have not been going the way he has envisioned them recently. On Tatooine, Luke is able to get a lead on Boba Fett's whereabouts. On [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], the near-dead [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] converses with [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who assures him that Luke will arrive. But it is not to be, and Yoda passes into the Force. Palpatine, Vader and Luke feel this at the same time, Luke departing [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to head to Dagobah to find out what happened, while Palpatine orders Vader to do the same. Arriving on Dagobah, Luke finds that Yoda has died. But both Yoda and Obi-Wan appear before him. The ghosts confirm Vader's claim that he is Luke's father and reveals that Leia is Luke's sister. They tell him that he must face Vader. When Luke refuses, they say that if he cannot, then Leia must. Luke, not willing to risk her life, finally agrees. Realizing that he has to confront Vader on his own, Luke has R2 record a message for Leia, then sends the message to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. Meanwhile, Leia, Chewbacca and Lando have tracked Boba Fett to an [[unidentified forested planet]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where he intends to deliver the carbonite block of Han back into [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] hands. Leia and the others managed to prevent this, killing Fett in the [[Showdown on unidentified forested planet|skirmish]]. They then leave the planet (with Leia piloting [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]) and head to the Rebel rendezvous at [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. Departing Dagobah, Luke is captured by a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and transported to the Second Death Star in orbit over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and is brought before the Emperor. Palpatine reveals to Luke that he knows that Luke hopes that his friends will not come to rescue him, then showing him what they will expect when they do arrive: a massed Imperial fleet. [[File:Lando-infinities.jpg|thumb|left|165x165px|Lando prepares for an assault on the second Death Star.]] On the [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], Han is finally unfrozen from the carbonite. However, the medics tell Leia that due to the long period of carbon freeze and damage to the controls, it is unlikely that he will ever regain his eyesight. Lando then prepares the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Falcon]] for an assault on the second Death Star that is scheduled to commence when the station's energy shield is destroyed by a Rebel unit on Endor's moon. Asked to come to the war room by [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and before the fleet leaves for Endor, the group listens to Luke's message, saying that Leia is his sister and that Vader is their father. They also find out that he has been captured and taken to the Death Star and that no rescue attempt is to be made. Meanwhile, a team inserted onto Endor make their way into the shield generator bunker. Suddenly, a massive group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST's]] arrive, forcing the remainder of the troops to attack. Back onboard the [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], Leia steals ''Slave I'' and heads for the Death Star on the Endor system to try and rescue Luke. Left with no other option, the entire fleet enters [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and follows her. Han realizes what has happened and asks Lando if he can accompany him and Chewbacca on the Falcon. [[File:Blindhan.jpg|thumb|right|220x220px|A blind Han Solo takes gunner seat aboard the Millennium Falcon.]] Arriving on the Death Star via [[Gate C-23]], Leia fights her way past the guards, but is eventually subdued and brought before Palpatine. She arrives in time to watch as the Alliance fleet arrives and is ambushed by the waiting Imperial fleet while waiting for the ground assault team to destroy the shield generator. The [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] has begun! On the forest moon, the fighting between the Rebels and Imperials draws close to [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], upsetting the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] there. As the Rebels make a suicide attack on the shield bunker, the Ewoks spring into action, attacking both sides. With the shield generator damaged, the ''Falcon'' heads for the moon's surface and, with a still blind Han taking Chewbacca's place in manning the quad turrets after Chewbacca is injured by pursuing [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], they take out the shield generator dish. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] leads several [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] inside the Death Star, where they destroy the main reactor. Lando takes the Falcon back to the Death Star to rescue Luke and Leia. [[File:WhiteVader.jpg|thumb|left|The redeemed Anakin Skywalker.|345x345px]]Onboard the Death Star, Palpatine orders Vader to kill Leia, but is stopped by Luke, who engages in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel. Vader overpowers Luke with the Emperor telling him to join them or die. Leia attempts to intervene, but is subdued by [[Force lightning/Legends|Force Lightning]] from the Emperor. Unswayed by the Emperor's proposal, Luke reveals to Vader that he and Leia are both his children. Vader is stunned by the revelation but says it is too late for him allowing Luke and Vader's duel to continue. Vader is overpowered by Luke, losing an arm in the process. But still, the Dark Lord is powerful enough to drive Luke to his knees. Luke boldly declares that he will not fight Vader anymore, and says that if he wants to strike both him and Leia down, he should do so now. Vader falls to his knees and begs for forgiveness, causing him to turn from the dark side of the Force and become Anakin Skywalker once again. The station is rocked with a huge explosion. As the station's crew begin to evacuate, the Emperor escapes, Luke and Leia, carrying their wounded father, make their way to the hangar bay, where Lando and the ''Falcon'' awaits them. The entire group makes it off the station before its destruction. Shortly thereafter, the entire group meets onboard the ''Home One''. Although the Death Star has been destroyed, Luke informs Mon Mothma that Palpatine has disappeared and is still a threat. The heroes, including a redeemed [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] (a Jedi once more and now clad in an all-white version of the life-support suit he wore as Vader), agree to help the Rebels to hunt him down, with Luke saying the Emperor doesn't stand a chance. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi}} ===Issues=== *[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 1|''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi'' 1]] *[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 2|''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi'' 2]] *[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 3|''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi'' 3]] *[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi 4|''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi'' 4]] ===Collected in=== *[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi (trade paperback)|''Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi'' trade paperback]] *''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Infinities]]'' *''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Infinities]]'' *''[[Star Wars Legends: The Rebellion Omnibus Vol. 3]]'' ==Sources== *{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20030807.html|text=November 2003 Comics}} *{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20030910.html|text=December 2003 Comics}} *{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20031008.html|text=January 2004 Comics}} *{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20031110.html|text=February 2004 Comics}} *{{SW|url=news/lego-star-wars-rebuild-the-galaxy-easter-eggs|text=Behind the Bricks: Our Favorite Easter Eggs from ''LEGO Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy''}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Die Rückkehr der Jedi-Ritter (Infinities) |es=Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi |ka=ვარსკვლავური ომები: უსასრულობები: ჯედაის დაბრუნება |pl=Infinities: Return of the Jedi }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Dark Horse Comics comic miniseries]] [[Category:Infinities]] [[Category:Non-canon comic miniseries]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi issues]] }}
